title: Tapping for the Evening Eating Spiral
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the 9pm pull toward food that is not hunger
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the evening eating spiral from Day 5 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program. The script addresses the pull toward food in the evenings that is not about hunger, and the guilt loop that follows.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 5 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the evenings when the pull toward food arrives before you are hungry, or when the guilt loop after eating is running. Good after dinner when the hand-to-mouth reach starts again.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 5 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS program. The script addresses the evening eating spiral: the pull toward food that arrives in the quiet of the evening and is not about hunger, and the guilt that follows after.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though the evening pull toward food is back and I know I am not hungry, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I eat in the evenings and then feel bad about it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I do not know what the evening eating is really about, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** The evening pull toward food is here again.
**Side of eye:** It is not hunger. I know it is not hunger.
**Under eye:** Something needs soothing and food is easy.
**Under nose:** The day has been a lot and now the evening is here.
**Chin:** I reach for food and then I feel bad.
**Collarbone:** Then I reach again because I feel bad.
**Under arm:** The loop runs most evenings and I am tired of it.
**Top of head:** I want to stop the spiral, not just stop the eating.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** The evening pull is a signal. I can listen to the signal.
**Side of eye:** What am I actually looking for in the evening?
**Under eye:** Rest. Quiet. Something of my own.
**Under nose:** I can give myself that without the food.
**Chin:** I can break the loop by meeting the need underneath it.
**Collarbone:** My evenings are allowed to be calm.
**Under arm:** I can learn what the evening actually needs.
**Top of head:** One evening at a time. Tonight I am listening.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 5 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
